topic: Workflow Architecture for SACCO Business Processes
questionsAsked: Business processes only vs both business + dev workflows? → Business processes only
Biggest pain point: silent failures, unclear state transitions, or hard to add new flows? → All of the above
Target scale: small SACCO vs multi-SACCO platform? → Single small SACCO (< 500 members)
Open to external workflow engine or Spring-only? → Spring-only
constraints: Spring Boot 3.4 monolith, no external workflow engines
All monetary operations require double-entry GL entries
Existing: 18 event types, 10 GL handlers, batch processing with idempotency log

candidates.name: Domain State Guards + Event Hardening
candidates.summary: Harden existing event-driven architecture with transactional outbox, dead letter queue, retry mechanism, idempotency keys, and correlation IDs. Add explicit state transition guards on entities (allowed transitions map) without a framework.
risks: Outbox processor adds ~5s eventual consistency delay (acceptable for small SACCO)
Dead letter retry could cause duplicates without idempotency keys
State guards on entities may feel scattered (mitigated by single ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS map)
mvp: Add state transition guard to LoanApplication entity
Create event_outbox + event_dead_letter tables via Liquibase
Build dead letter writer and @Scheduled retry job
Add correlation ID and idempotency key to AuditableEvent interface

candidates.name: Lifecycle State Machine Module
candidates.summary: Build a generic StateMachine<S,E> abstraction in sacco-common. Define concrete state machines for loan, payout, and contribution lifecycles with explicit states, transitions, guards (predicates), and actions (side effects). Includes transition audit logging and event hardening.
risks: Over-engineering for a small system (mitigated: minimal abstraction < 200 lines)
Requires refactoring existing services to use state machine API
Guard predicates may need service-layer dependencies (mitigated: context objects)
mvp: Build generic StateMachine abstraction in sacco-common/workflow/
Define LoanLifecycle state machine with all 7 states and transitions
Refactor LoanServiceImpl to delegate status changes to state machine

candidates.name: Process Template + Event Catalog
candidates.summary: Create an annotation-based business process registry and event catalog. @BusinessProcess annotations declare triggers, steps, published events, and GL requirements. EventCatalog scanner validates GL completeness at test time. Focuses on discoverability and consistency for adding new flows.
risks: Annotations add ceremony without runtime behavior
ProcessTemplate may be too abstract to be useful
Doesn't solve runtime state transition or failure problems alone
mvp: Build EventCatalog scanner that maps events to handlers
Write integration test verifying GL completeness
Add event hardening from Candidate A

recommendation.primaryRationale: Addresses the most critical pain point (silent failures / GL drift) immediately. Lowest risk with additive changes. Entity-level state guards solve 80% of unclear transitions with 20% effort. Outbox + dead letter is a well-understood reliability pattern. Proportional to a single small SACCO.
recommendation.backupRationale: Consider upgrading to this for the loan module specifically after event hardening is in place. The loan lifecycle has 7 states with complex business rules that benefit from formal state machine clarity and testability.
suggestedSequence: Phase 1: Event hardening (outbox, dead letter, idempotency, correlation IDs)
Phase 2: Entity state guards (LoanApplication, PayoutRequest, ContributionStatus)
Phase 3 (optional): Formal loan state machine if guards prove insufficient
Phase 4 (optional): Event catalog + GL completeness test
